recentTweets.text: My statement on the Massie Amendment to the National Security Department of State, & Related Programs Appropriations Bill https://t.co/IHkP43FMSp

Today is the 4th anniversary of the Bipartisan Safer Communities Act, the first federal gun violence prevention legislation to become law in over 30 years.

We must keep building on this foundation to keep communities like North Carolina's 12th District safe. https://t.co/Ln1TI5gZ2y

Gun violence is the leading cause of death of American children and teens.
As Gun Violence Prevention Month comes to an end, please remember to help keep our community safe by securely storing firearms. Safer storage can save lives. https://t.co/SJdQKfp1Ms
https://t.co/60rTj3iJVR

I voted for the Iran War Powers Resolution.

For over 90 days, the Trump administration has waged war on Iran without a defined exit strategy or congressional authorization, dragging our country further into Trump’s self-declared war of choice and a deepening financial crisis. https://t.co/7TNua3ZO73
I am working in the Education and Workforce Committee to ensure we prioritize students and educators over the interests of AI companies. https://t.co/O0Q885L123

Wade’s award-winning piece, African Basquiat, an Acrylic and Polly paint masterpiece was unveiled during the Grand Opening Exhibition Sunday, May 17, 2026, at Mint Museum Uptown at Levine Center for the Arts in Charlotte. https://t.co/kk9gNpciCl

I am proud to announce Morgan Wade, a sophomore at West Charlotte High School, as the winner of the 2026 Congressional Art Competition representing North Carolina’s 12th Congressional District. https://t.co/ewRjYYVHbF

Requiring proof of intent to discriminate in states with documented histories of racial voter suppression is not legal interpretation. It is judicial activism and willful blindness. https://t.co/1jeKnijNml
Congressional oversight exists to serve the public good, not to strong arm universities and police students' political opinions. https://t.co/ztHX9YsGSG
This Farm Bill hurts farmers and consumers and it fails to address the current problems facing our farms.
Food assistance is getting cut for those who need it. Costs of fertilizer, equipment, fuel, and labor are rising, tariffs are swinging up and down, crop prices are weak, and farms are going under.
This Farm Bill is just about Punishing Poor People. Call your member of Congress and let them know it's a BAD BILL. https://t.co/UQKJdlWJMQ
The exhibition will remain on view in the Mint Museum’s Uptown Culbertson Family Learning Center from May 17 through June 7.

The first-place winner will have their artwork displayed in the U.S. Capitol for one year and will be invited, along with a guardian, to Washington, D.C. to participate in the official installation ceremony.

Congresswoman Alma S. Adams, Ph.D. (NC-12) is proud to present the grand opening of the 2026 12th District Congressional Art Competition Exhibition, celebrating the creativity, talent, and artistic vision of the next generation of artists from North Carolina’s 12th District. https://t.co/rGWqlz8wlA

The portrait of April Valentine outside my office honors a young mother who lost her life to an untreated blood clot during pregnancy.

The CDC has recognized that 80% of maternal deaths are preventable, and Black women are disproportionately affected.

This Black Maternal Health Week, Congress must take meaningful action and push for the systemic changes Black moms deserve.

#inherhonor
#justice4April
#ariahfoundation
#BMHW

The Congressional Mamas’ Caucus partnered with Mothering Justice and the Ariah Foundation to create the In Her Honor portrait series to recognize the women of color that have died due to maternal health complications. Their names and stories demand action. https://t.co/NyCKCjBLKr

Today, I introduced a resolution to recognize April 11 to April 17 as Black Maternal Health Week with Congresswoman Lauren Underwood and Senator Cory Booker.

You can read more about it on my website, https://t.co/oT9QyqxFrp https://t.co/a3mXe3bsDf
It's Tax Day. Don’t forget to file by 11:59 PM tonight! https://t.co/WXqR0wnDp8
Happy Vaisakhi! May this celebration of faith and abundance remind us all of the importance of the Sikh values of charity, unity, and justice. https://t.co/ZkkR09qu3q

Medical and dental residents shouldn't have to watch their student loan debt climb while they're training to serve our community.

I am proud to co-sponsor the bipartisan REDI Act to stop interest from accruing during residency and help address the growing doctor shortage. https://t.co/GB8tYq7C8Q
Their work in signals intelligence was vital but largely classified. It is long past time that we honor these veterans and thank them for their service.

Last Thursday, I introduced HR. 8211, the Army Security Agency Monument Act.

This bill would establish a monument at Arlington National Cemetery honoring the men and women of the U.S. Army Security Agency who served during WWII, the Korean War, the Vietnam War, and the Cold War. https://t.co/xdiraRilMI

During Black Maternal Health Week, we raise awareness of and take action to end our maternal health crisis. Black women are three times more likely to die from a pregnancy-related cause. The #Momnibus is the solution. https://t.co/nNF5v5VobX

I am proud to work with Representatives Cohen, Johnson, and Levin by co-sponsoring an amendment to the Constitution limiting the power of presidential pardons with House Joint Resolution 13. https://t.co/g889Ou5S45
We should not be at war with Iran. https://t.co/sJ6Dj4RnBz
At the same time, concerns remain about actions tied to the Trump administration—especially policies impacting immigrant communities and federal workers.

This is about accountability.
This is about people.
This is about making government work for those who need it most. 

Stay informed. Stay engaged. Stay safe.

What’s a discharge petition—and why does it matter right now? 

Alma Adams breaks it down. https://t.co/VWfuN7vZBk

When fertilizer prices go up, so do your grocery bills. This a…

Happy 135th Founders Day to my alma mater, NC A&T State University.

Our university has shaped generations of leaders who challenge the status quo.

We celebrate the legacy of our founders and the pride that runs through every student, faculty member and graduate. Aggie Pride!

Today's attack on Iran was executed without Congressional authorization.

Congress must exercise its constitutional power and return to Washington immediately. https://t.co/ZMCTZGIPmW
Wishing everyone in Charlotte a happy and safe Mardi Gras. Laissez les bons temps rouler… let the good times roll! https://t.co/taanasQqDW
Ramadan is a sacred time for reflection and prayer. Ramadan Mubarak to all observing in the NC 12th district! https://t.co/ZJLvxX67nT
Happy Pączki Day! This Polish-American tradition has become a delicious way to mark the start of the Lenten season. What’s your favorite flavor? https://t.co/1NoeDEfYec

RT @HouseAgDems: “This is not good faith negotiation." 

This is a partisan, Republican farm bill that ignores the biggest crises in farm c…
Cancer is a leading cause of death worldwide. On World Cancer Day, we remember the lives lost to this deadly disease and honor their memory by striving to find cures. https://t.co/WNqHlEssuB
Today I voted against H.R. 7148, a bill that would direct funding to the Department of Homeland Security. https://t.co/NKb92BHjCL

100 years ago, Dr. Carter G. Woodson established Negro History Week to recognize the contributions that Black people have made to American life and culture. Today and every day, we commit to telling the full story of the American journey. https://t.co/8XnmwimGNQ

The killing of Alex Pretti by a Customs and Border Patrol agent in Minneapolis is horrifying.

This needs to end. https://t.co/tzGCCNx39n
Today, our community pays tribute to the memory of the late Reverend Doctor Martin Luther King Jr. by remembering his unshakable faith in a more just, equitable society. https://t.co/o7F8VG54xM

Make no mistake, this threat to cancel the midterms isn’t a joke.

President Trump already staged an insurrection once to overturn the 2020 election—we can’t allow it to happen again. 

Every member of Congress must stand opposed to this dangerous, anti-democratic rhetoric.

I have introduced impeachment articles alongside 70 House Democrats against DHS Sec. Kristi Noem.

I do not take impeachment lightly but it is clear that Sec. Noem has violated federal law and her oath of office and, for the safety of our country, she must be removed from office. https://t.co/lobnHPYywk
RT @HouseAgDems: Trump promised to put America first. Instead, his tariffs drove up grocery prices for every American, killed export market…
To the First, Foremost and Finest, Happy Founders' Day to all my Sorors! I'm proud of 118 years of Alpha Kappa Alpha excellence. #AKA https://t.co/h9CBkbmbeB

Yesterday, I spoke on the House floor about how dismantling the Department of Education is bad for North Carolina's children.

Over 70% of CMS students benefit directly from programs administered through the Department.

Protecting the ED means protecting opportunity. https://t.co/pzneES4dQa

I'm calling for an independent investigation into the fatal shooting of Renee Nicole Good in Minneapolis by a masked ICE agent.

Read my full statement here: https://t.co/x5jZSPlDWT https://t.co/UKV0BkqFcs

My statement on the successful bipartisan House vote to renew the ACA tax credits and provide much-needed relief to the people of Charlotte-Mecklenburg.

Read my full statement here: https://t.co/b18p7PoViO https://t.co/dhqkXAs9Yb

In fact, as of 2023, 26% of single-family homes in Charlotte were owned by corporations—it’s unacceptable!

My bill will increase housing supply, lower costs, and make it easier for Americans purchase a home. Let’s get to work delivering housing relief for our communities.
Learn more about the American Neighborhoods Protection Act here: https://t.co/c61GpIaJlb

It seems like the president is a fan of my bill, the American Neighborhoods Protection Act.

A home should be a place for you to start a family and build your life, but corporations are buying up the housing supply, preventing Americans from reaching the American dream. https://t.co/xdp3T7PNwa

January 6th, 2021 was an insurrection caused by Donald Trump. I was here with my staff as it happened.

It was a black mark on our history but one we cannot forget and cannot allow to happen again. https://t.co/9YeiXgqnZV

Five years ago today, Donald Trump incited a violent mob to brutally attack the U.S. Capitol on January 6.

Their mission was to overturn a free and fair election.

We will never allow extremists to whitewash their treachery. https://t.co/UZeiHRr8Wi
My statement on the Trump administration's unauthorized strike on Venezuela and President Trump's utter violation of the U.S. Constitution. https://t.co/7S4NxDVdq9

Happy New Year! I'm wishing all my constituents a blessed, safe, and joyous 2026.

As we enter this new year, let us remember to lend a helping hand to those in need, show empathy to those who are hurting, and, most of all, spread love wherever we go. https://t.co/FE2XLdD17q

Merry Christmas!

I hope today brings you love, family, friendship, merriment, and grace as we celebrate the birth of our savior. May the love of Christ fill your home with warmth and bring peace to your heart. https://t.co/aJhVnwd1VH

Gov. Hunt was one of the greatest governors to ever serve North Carolina. His compassion, care, & commitment to our state will be felt for generations to come.

My prayers go out to his family, friends, & loved ones. Gov. Hunt will be missed but his legacy will never be forgotten

As America suffers from the Republican healthcare and affordability crisis, the Republican solution is to make healthcare more expensive and ensure less people have access to it.

Read my full statement here: https://t.co/KICklePzKx https://t.co/XDcI7m3reN

I hope today brings you love, family, friendship, merriment, and grace as we celebrate the birth of our savior. May the love of Christ fill your home with warmth and bring peace to your heart. https://t.co/aJhVnwd1VH

My prayers go out to his family, friends, & loved ones. Gov. Hunt will be missed but his legacy will never be forgotten

As America suffers from the Republican healthcare and affordability crisis, the Republican solution is to make healthcare more expensive and ensure less people have access to it.

Read my full statement here: https://t.co/KICklePzKx https://t.co/XDcI7m3reN

The House Republican bill is bad for Americans.

It fails to extend the ACA tax credits. It abandons financial assistance for middle class families. It strips away protections for patients.

People are dying and it’s time my Republican colleagues take this crisis seriously. https://t.co/KleZzF5dSj

Western NC needs federal assistance, not red tape delaying the support they are owed.

I'm proud to join my NC Democratic colleagues in introducing the FEMA Administrative Reform Act to speed up federal relief dollars to our state.

Learn more here: https://t.co/iICJOcZoeR https://t.co/064fUZgeL4

I was proud to join @SenMarkey in introducing the EFFECTIVE Food Procurement Act to build sustainability and equity in the USDA's food purchases.

When our small-scale & underserved producers are empowered to succeed, everyone wins and our country becomes stronger because of it. https://t.co/sxmwGAw55Q
Wishing our Charlotte-Mecklenburg Jewish community a happy and blessed Hanukkah filled with love and light! Chag sameach! https://t.co/fdJBgAWRuU

Our students deserve transparency when knowing the cost of their education.

I'm proud that my amendment requiring the Department of Education to give greater clarity around tuition calculation costs was unanimously approved by the Education and Workforce Committee. https://t.co/P9yzAEHtuU

On International Human Rights Day, I'm proud to be a co-lead on @RepNikema's resolution affirming reproductive freedom and abortion care as a human right.

Abortion care is healthcare and women can’t be free without their right to reproductive freedom. https://t.co/lgvGc6vilH

RT @RepDeborahRoss: Today I led my NC colleagues in demanding Speaker Mike Johnson provide his plan to avoid a health care crisis.

With th…

You are cordially invited to join me for my 2026 State of the District on Thursday, January 29, 2026, at 7:00 PM!

RSVP here to receive updates about the event: https://t.co/kFutXuP6Do https://t.co/KW1PZRHlNB

On this day in 1865, the 13th Amendment abolished slavery in the United States, a first step in the long fight for equality for African Americans.

We must never forget the horrors of slavery and must always reject every attempt to erase this shameful chapter of American history.

On December 5, 1955, the Montgomery Bus Boycott began. We remember and honor the legacy of the Dr. Martin Luther King, Jr., Rosa Parks, and many other civil rights heroes who were helped lead this momentous movement.
I took to the House floor tonight to speak against the campaign of terror the Department of Homeland Security brought to Charlotte last month. https://t.co/4exNQT0akV

On this day 70 years ago, Rosa Parks refused to give up her bus seat in Montgomery, courageously fighting against injustice in the pursuit of civil rights.

Her act was a catalyst for change and her life of service continues to inspire us to stand up for justice and equality.

I’m heartbroken to hear about the passing of Specialist Sarah Beckstrom after yesterday’s shooting in Washington, DC.

I’m keeping her family and loved ones in my prayers during this difficult time, and I continue to pray for Staff Sgt. Andrew Wolfe’s recovery.

As we gather with loved ones today, I want to wish you a happy Thanksgiving.

May this holiday bring warmth to your homes, joy to your hearts, love for your friends and family, and a renewed sense of care for our community. https://t.co/WRPoxyLf3D

My prayers are with the National Guardsmen wounded in the horrific shooting in Washington, DC today.

I’m thankful for the first responders who quickly secured the scene and apprehended the suspect.

Violence has no place in America.

Today, I contacted the Department of Homeland Security and demanded answers for Border Patrol's disturbing actions in Charlotte.

This campaign of terror will have long-term and devastating effects on the Charlotte community and my constituents deserve transparency. https://t.co/N2Eh7NJ34c
My statement on U.S. Customs and Border Patrol ending their campaign of terror in Charlotte. https://t.co/DQ9Rh60Bpv

21% of all CMS students missed school on Monday after the Trump administration deployed Border Patrol agents to our community.

Students & parents are afraid to use their constitutionally protected right to a high quality education because of Trump's campaign of terror. https://t.co/BvbQ4koyke

The victims of Jeffrey Epstein, their families, and the American people deserve to know the whole truth about Epstein’s heinous crimes.

I was proud to vote for the release of the Epstein files today because justice cannot be served in darkness. https://t.co/vEZ90A5viJ

Charlotte, our community is under siege.

If there are things you see from Border Patrol that should not be, please report those incidents to my office immediately and please be careful.

Call my Charlotte office at (704) 344-9950 or my Washington, DC office at (202) 225-1510. https://t.co/y9H3crhx6T
My statement on U.S. Border Patrol and ICE agents being deployed in Charlotte. https://t.co/mg0leRSa4S
https://t.co/J47IALNJpt

I cannot support a Continuing Resolution that doesn't meet the healthcare needs of my constituents.

I grew up without healthcare. Not only is it important, it's necessary. Make no mistake, we're going to continue this fight because healthcare isn't a luxury, it's a human right. https://t.co/2fRdKySUwe

This #VeteransDay, we celebrate the brave men and women who served our country in uniform. 

Our freedoms rest on your shoulders, your sacrifice, and those of your comrades who never came home. 

On behalf of all the residents of the 12th District, thank you for your service.

I want to thank our food banks and food pantries for continuing to step up and meet the moment for our community. These organizations were on the front lines while hundreds of thousands of North Carolinians were unsure where their next meal was coming from.

This funding was only administered because Democrats sued the USDA to release SNAP benefits and stop holding the American people hostage. 

This is far from the last fight SNAP faces and Democrats are ready to defend the lifesaving program.

I am proud to announce that my constituents will receive their full SNAP benefits in November.

After weeks of fighting the Trump administration’s unlawful actions, help is finally on the way for 1.4 million North Carolinians who rely on SNAP to make the ends meet. https://t.co/HbWrXMmXPc

Thank you to @handhfoodpantry for your work keeping our community fed.

Access to food is a basic human right and, during this critical time for SNAP, our food pantries are on the front lines of making sure Charlotte-Mecklenburg families are able to put food on their tables. https://t.co/XXFK8tzTBP

I said during my "Hunger Matters" webinar that hunger, housing, and healthcare should not be partisan issues. I stand by that.
These are not unique to red states or blue states. It's a red, white, and blue issue. It's an American issue. https://t.co/jamGBt0q18

The deadline to submit your application to my office for the 2025 Military Service Academy nominations is this Friday, November 7, 2025 at 5:00 PM.

My thoughts and prayers go out to the family, friends, and loved ones of former Vice President Dick Cheney.

I was proud to join @nourishupclt and @shmetrolina for today's Pop Up Food Share event, handing out food to hundreds of Charlotte-Mecklenburg families.
